steps.title: Use the tangent condition
steps.content: A circle intersects the $y$-axis at exactly one point when it is tangent to the $y$-axis. That happens when the distance from the center to the $y$-axis equals the radius.

steps.title: Compare center distance and radius
steps.content: Each choice has radius $5$ because $r^2=25$. Choice C has center $(5,12)$, whose distance to the $y$-axis is $5$. That equals the radius, so the circle touches the $y$-axis once. The answer is C.
